WTI.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $80.54 vs history $84.88 diverge by 5.1%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: Monday's session saw oil prices plunge 4.6% to $80.97 after President Trump announced a completed peace deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, sending the VIX down 9.1% to 17.68. US equities edged higher (SPY +0.31%, QQQ +0.27%), but the relief rally was tempered by a modest rise in the 10-year yield to 4.49% ahead of Chair Warsh's first FOMC meeting this week. Semiconductors were a mixed bag: SOXX rose 0.89%, with large-cap names like AMD (+4%) and INTC (+5.1%) surging on foundry and upgrade news, while NVDA (-0.38%) and AVGO (-0.85%) drifted as the cluster divergence deepened.
commodities_note: WTI data shows conflicting sources: fast_info prev_close $80.54 vs history $84.88 (5.1% discrepancy). The daily decline is unambiguous, but the absolute level is uncertain — use 5d (-11.3%) and 1mo (-20.0%) trends as reliable signals. Gold edged down 0.3% to $4,333 as safe-haven demand faded. The 10-year yield rose 2.4bp to 4.49%, suggesting rate-hike fears persist despite peace hopes.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: Nearest support zone

thesis_highlights: WeRide Q1 2026 results (reported May 13 2026): revenue +58% YoY on Robotaxi fleet expansion and L2++ ADAS commercialisation — cite the print specifics when news references it.
WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS, but momentum_warn is True (weak recent tape). ACCUMULATE requires either an explicit multi-year structural support cite (200-day SMA / prior cycle low / named floor) OR an imminent hard catalyst within 7 days (specific date). Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

writeup.headline: WeRide -3.1% further into oversold — R:R 5.7:1, but no structural support.
writeup.what_to_do: WRD fell another 3.1% Monday, RSI at 34.1, and the 5.7:1 R:R using support at 6.03 is attractive on paper. However, no 200-day or structural floor exists, and the ADR/delisting risk persists. The robotaxi deals in Europe are real, but cash burn is high. Upgrades to WATCH on a volume spike above 6.50 with a stop at 6.03; avoid below 6.03.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks drifted higher Monday, but the NIM compression theme persists with US10Y at 4.49% (far above the 4.20% reset). DBS, OCBC, and UOB are all extended above their 50-days, with volume thinning; only UOB shows value at a 20% discount to cluster median P/E. The cluster remains in a waiting pattern for either a US10Y break below 4.20% or MAS easing, which isn't expected until October.
singapore.key_development: Oil's slide below $81 temporarily eases one channel of NIM pressure, but the US10Y remains elevated and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster finished 0.9% higher as peace-deal relief lifted AMD and INTC, while NVDA and AVGO dribbled lower. The extreme extension across the group is severe: 11 of 27 tickers hard-blocked, with SOXX +10.5% in 5 days. Intel's foundry thesis is the unique catalyst, but its RCP phase means no entry yet. The cluster faces a binary FOMC this week — if Chair Warsh signals rate hikes, extended semis will be the first hit. Wait for a pullback to the 50-day across the cluster before sizing.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — wait for pullback
semiconductors.key_development: AMD +4% on Citi upgrade and INTC +5% on foundry momentum, while MU's extreme extension hardens ahead of June 25 earnings.

big_tech.summary: BigTech was mixed Monday, with AMZN and MSFT weak, GOOG flat, PLTR pulling back. The cluster's median PE is 24.4x, and only GOOG and AMZN have the earnings power to support that multiple near-term. MSFT's OpenAI concentration risk and PLTR's legal overhang weigh. The next catalyst is after earnings season in late July, leaving the group range-bound into FOMC.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk lingers
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's RSI hit 37 as the stock fell 0.5% Monday, and AVGO's post-earnings drift continued.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium, Google Axion) directly compete with AVGO and NVDA — hyperscaler custom silicon trend benefits Marvell.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL's data center revenue growth exceeds 40% in next earnings, or on any pullback to its 50-day.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron — Samsung's qualification progress with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung announces HBM3E qualification with NVDA, or on a 10% pullback from current levels.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit Arista; NOK's AI networking thesis shares read-through on hyperscaler switch spend.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist on a 5-7% pullback from recent highs, or if Q2 earnings show 30%+ growth in data center switching.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment partner alongside LITE — direct beneficiary of any AI optics capacity expansion.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Greensboro fab progress signals a supply chain bottleneck, or on a pullback to its 50-day.

active_risks: US-Iran peace deal announced but implementation uncertain — Strait of Hormuz reopening may take months, with 100M barrels of stranded crude still to clear.
China tech blacklist expansion and indium phosphide export controls tighten semiconductor supply chain, risking AI datacenter buildouts.
FOMC decision this week (June 18) — Chair Warsh faces pressure to hold rates, but inflation at 4.2% keeps a hawkish bias; a hike signal could roil markets.
French sovereign debt crisis looms as energy-price shock, ECB rate hikes, and political uncertainty converge, threatening Eurozone financial stability.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil's collapse below $81 removes the energy-cost tailwind that lifted energy stocks and pushed up inflation expectations. The VIX plunging to 17.68 suggests a ceasefire rally, but the FOMC's hawkish lean could quickly reverse that. Semis are the most vulnerable: SOXX +10.5% in a week leaves them exposed to any rate-hike signal. AVGO and MSFT, below their 50-days, may find relative safety.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: President Trump announced an Iran peace deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz (per CNBC/Ynetnews, June 15). Oil plunged 4.6% on the news. Also, the US imposed an export ban on foreign access to new Anthropic AI models (per Newsweek, June 14).

base.description: Peace deal implementation proceeds slowly, Strait reopens by July, WTI consolidates around $75-80, Fed holds rates steady; semis consolidate gains and AVGO/MSFT reclaim 50-days.

optimistic.description: Peace deal takes hold faster than expected, oil drops to $65, inflation eases, Fed cuts in Q4; semis rally and ASML, MU retrace to buyable levels.

pessimistic.description: Peace deal collapses, Iran resumes attacks, oil surges back above $95, Fed forced to hike rates; semis sell off, all CAUTION tickers break below 200-days.

wildcard.description: Anthropic export ban triggers a broader AI model trade war between US and China, collapsing AI capex and sending NVDA down 20%; defensive names like SG banks benefit.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash through FOMC; no new entries until rate trajectory clarifies. Only after Warsh's statement should you consider nibbling at oversold names like AVGO or MSFT.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously defensive — waiting for FOMC clarity
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 11 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The peace-deal rally lifted semis but the extreme extension and pre-FOMC uncertainty make fresh entry dangerous. Wait for a corrective pullback.

Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=10) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
